skill.name: conductor-status
skill.description: Display project status, active tracks, and next actions
skill.summary: Display project status, active tracks, and next actions
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 61 potential issues (external_commands, weak crypto, system reconnaissance) but manual review confirms these are false positives. The skill is a legitimate project management tool that reads local markdown and JSON files to display project status. No actual command execution, cryptographic operations, or network requests are performed.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged backticks in markdown as Ruby/shell command execution. Actual content is regex patterns like `/^- \[x\] Task/` used for task counting documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Regex delimiters in documentation are not executable shell commands

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown task list markers [x], [~], [ ] as weak cryptographic algorithms or array syntax.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Markdown task list syntax is not cryptographic code

content.value_statement: Conductor-status displays project progress, active tracks, and next actions in a formatted dashboard. It helps teams stay organized by tracking tasks across multiple work streams.
actual_capabilities: Display overall project progress with percentage completion
Show individual track status (pending, in-progress, complete)
Identify current focus and next actions for each track
Detect and display blockers between dependent tracks
limitations: Requires Conductor to be initialized with conductor:setup
Only reads local files in conductor/ directory structure
Cannot create or modify tracks (use other Conductor skills)
Does not integrate with external project management tools

use_cases.title: Daily Standup Updates
use_cases.description: Quickly review project progress and identify blockers before team meetings

use_cases.title: Progress Reporting
use_cases.description: Generate formatted status reports for stakeholders with detailed track breakdowns

use_cases.title: Workflow Navigation
use_cases.description: Identify the next task to work on based on current track phases and priorities

prompt_templates.title: Quick Status Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-status to show the current project status. What is the overall progress percentage and which track is currently active?
prompt_templates.scenario: Get a rapid overview of project health

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Track Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Run conductor-status with the track ID [track-id] to see the detailed status of a specific track including its phase progress and next steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a specific track in detail

prompt_templates.title: Identify Blockers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-status to check if there are any blockers in the project. What tracks are blocked and what are they waiting on?

prompt_templates.title: JSON Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Run conductor-status --json to get the full project status in JSON format for integration with other tools.

output_examples.output:
PROJECT STATUS: MyProject
Last Updated: 2025-01-15

Tracks: 3/5 completed (60%)
Tasks: 15/25 completed (60%)

Active Track: feature_dashboard - Dashboard Feature
Current Phase: Phase 2: Core Components
Current Task: [~] Task 2.3: Implement chart rendering

TRACK STATUS: Authentication Feature
Track ID: auth_feature
Type: feature
Status: In Progress

Overall: 8/12 tasks (67%)

Phase 2: API Integration [IN PROGRESS]
  - [x] Task 2.1: Create auth endpoints
  - [~] Task 2.2: Add token validation  <-- CURRENT
  - [ ] Task 2.3: Implement logout

best_practices: Run conductor-status before starting new work to understand current project state
Use --quick mode for brief updates in team communications
Check for blockers regularly to avoid wasted effort on blocked tasks
anti_patterns: Running conductor-status without first initializing Conductor with conductor:setup
Expecting external tool integration when this skill only reads local files
Using conductor-status to modify tasks (this skill is read-only)

faq.question: What is Conductor?
faq.answer: Conductor is a project management framework that organizes work into tracks. Each track contains phases and tasks stored as local markdown files.

faq.question: How do I initialize Conductor?
faq.answer: Run /conductor:setup to create the initial conductor/ directory structure with product.md and tracks.md files.

faq.question: Can this skill modify tasks?
faq.answer: No, conductor-status is read-only. Use conductor:implement or other Conductor skills to modify task status.

faq.question: What do the status markers mean?
How do I see a specific track?
faq.answer: Pass the track ID as an argument: conductor-status [track-id]

faq.question: Can I export the status data?
faq.answer: Yes, use --json flag to get machine-readable output for integration with other tools.
